main.h(3) Library Functions Manual main.h(3)

NAME

main.h - ascii-chat Discovery Mode Entry Point Header

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int discovery_main (void)
Discovery mode entry point for unified binary.

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Discovery Mode Entry Point Header

This header exposes the discovery mode entry point for the unified binary architecture. The unified binary dispatches to discovery_main() when invoked as ascii-chat discovery.

Discovery Mode Philosophy

"ascii-chat should be as simple as making a phone call."

Discovery mode enables zero-configuration video chat:

  • One command to start: ascii-chat swift-river-mountain
  • Automatic host negotiation: Best NAT quality participant becomes host
  • Dynamic role switching: Participants can become hosts mid-session
  • Seamless failover: If host disconnects, another participant takes over

Mode Entry Point Contract

Each mode entry point (server_main, client_main, discovery_main) must:

  • Accept no arguments: int mode_main(void)
  • Options are already parsed by main dispatcher (available via GET_OPTION())
  • Return 0 on success, non-zero error code on failure
  • Perform mode-specific initialization and main loop
  • Perform complete cleanup before returning

Implementation Notes

Discovery mode combines client and server capabilities:

  • Uses session library (lib/session/) for capture, display, hosting
  • Connects to ACDS for session discovery and NAT quality assessment
  • Can transition between participant and host roles dynamically

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Version

1.0

Definition in file discovery/main.h.

Function Documentation

int discovery_main (void )

Discovery mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete discovery mode lifecycle:

  • Session discovery via ACDS or LAN mDNS
  • NAT quality assessment for host selection
  • Dynamic role switching (participant <-> host)
  • Media streaming (video/audio capture and display)
  • Graceful shutdown and cleanup

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher before this function is called, so they are available via GET_OPTION().

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Example

# Join a session (session string as positional argument):
ascii-chat discovery swift-river-mountain
# Or more concisely (session strings are auto-detected):
ascii-chat swift-river-mountain

Discovery mode entry point for unified binary.

Handles session discovery, host negotiation, and media flow.

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Definition at line 326 of file discovery/main.c.

326                          {
327   log_debug("discovery_main() starting");
328 
329   // Discovery-specific setup: initialize ACDS connection and session negotiation
330   // Note: Shared setup (keepawake, splash, terminal, capture, display, audio)
331   // is handled by session_client_like_run()
332 
333   // Get session string from options (command-line argument)
334   const char *session_string = GET_OPTION(session_string);
335   bool is_initiator = (session_string == NULL || session_string[0] == '\0');
336 
337   int port_int = GET_OPTION(port);
338 
339   log_debug("Discovery: is_initiator=%d, port=%d", is_initiator, port_int);
340 
341   // Validate mutually exclusive options: discovery_service_url vs discovery_port
342   const char *service_url = GET_OPTION(discovery_service_url);
343   if (service_url && service_url[0] != '\0') {
344     int discovery_port = GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
345     if (discovery_port != ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT) {
346       log_fatal("--discovery-service-url and --discovery-service-port are mutually exclusive");
347       return ERROR_USAGE;
348     }
349   }
350 
351   // Create discovery session configuration
352   discovery_config_t discovery_config = {
353       .acds_address = GET_OPTION(discovery_server),
354       .acds_port = (uint16_t)GET_OPTION(discovery_port),
355       .acds_url = service_url,
356       .session_string = is_initiator ? NULL : session_string,
357       .local_port = (uint16_t)port_int,
358       .on_state_change = on_discovery_state_change,
359       .on_session_ready = on_session_ready,
360       .on_error = on_discovery_error,
361       .callback_user_data = NULL,
362       .should_exit_callback = discovery_capture_should_exit_adapter,
363       .exit_callback_data = NULL,
364   };
365 
366   // Create discovery session (stored in global for discovery_run() to access)
367   g_discovery = discovery_session_create(&discovery_config);
368   if (!g_discovery) {
369     log_fatal("Failed to create discovery session");
370     return ERROR_MEMORY;
371   }
372 
373   // Start discovery session (connects to ACDS, creates/joins, initiates NAT negotiation)
374   log_debug("Discovery: starting discovery session");
375   asciichat_error_t result = discovery_session_start(g_discovery);
376   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
377     log_fatal("Failed to start discovery session: %d", result);
378     discovery_session_destroy(g_discovery);
379     g_discovery = NULL;
380     return result;
381   }
382 
383   // No network interrupt callback needed - discovery session handles its own shutdown
384   set_interrupt_callback(NULL);
385 
386   /* ========================================================================
387    * Configure and Run Shared Client-Like Session Framework
388    * ========================================================================
389    * session_client_like_run() handles all shared initialization:
390    * - Terminal output management
391    * - Keepawake system
392    * - Splash screen lifecycle
393    * - Media source selection
394    * - FPS probing
395    * - Audio initialization
396    * - Display context creation
397    * - Proper cleanup ordering
398    *
399    * Discovery mode provides:
400    * - discovery_run() callback: NAT negotiation, role determination, media handling
401    * - discovery_keyboard_handler: interactive controls for participant role
402    */
403 
404   session_client_like_config_t config = {
405       .run_fn = discovery_run,
406       .run_user_data = NULL,
407       .tcp_client = NULL,
408       .websocket_client = NULL,
409       .discovery = (void *)g_discovery, // Opaque pointer to discovery session
410       .custom_should_exit = NULL,
411       .exit_user_data = NULL,
412       .keyboard_handler = discovery_keyboard_handler,
413       .max_reconnect_attempts = 0, // Discovery doesn't retry - role is determined once
414       .should_reconnect_callback = NULL,
415       .reconnect_user_data = NULL,
416       .reconnect_delay_ms = 0,
417       .print_newline_on_tty_exit = false, // Server/participant manages cursor
418   };
419 
420   log_debug("Discovery: calling session_client_like_run()");
421   asciichat_error_t session_result = session_client_like_run(&config);
422   log_debug("Discovery: session_client_like_run() returned %d", session_result);
423 
424   // Cleanup discovery session
425   log_debug("Discovery: cleaning up");
426 
427   if (g_discovery) {
428     discovery_session_stop(g_discovery);
429     discovery_session_destroy(g_discovery);
430     g_discovery = NULL;
431   }
432 
433   return (session_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) ? 0 : (int)session_result;
434 }




References ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, discovery_session_create(),
    discovery_session_destroy(), discovery_session_start(),
    discovery_session_stop(), ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_USAGE,
    GET_OPTION, log_debug, log_fatal,
    session_client_like_run(), and set_interrupt_callback().
